one_line: Zero insider open-market buys in 12 months despite 40%+ crash; Berkshire fully exited 5M shares in Q1 2026; Viking Global dissolved its 1.2M-share stake — smart money sold into any recovery, not accumulating.

citations.fact: Zero insider purchases in past 12 months; only 2 sells: Optum CEO Conway sold 800 shares at $355 on 4/23/2026 ($284K) and Director Baker sold 27 shares at $356 on 9/11/2025 ($9.6K); insider ownership 0.28%
June 2026 Form 4s show only tax-withholding dispositions (non-discretionary) and routine option awards — no open-market buys by any executive or director despite stock trading near multi-year lows
Berkshire Hathaway Q1 2026 13F (filed 5/15/2026) shows full exit of UNH position — all ~5M shares sold after buying at ~$271 during the 2025 crisis; Berkshire exited 16 positions total in Q1 2026

Viking Global reduced UNH from peak of 2.1M shares (March 2025, ~$1.1B) to 1.2M shares (December 2025, $395M); MarketScreener headline confirms Viking subsequently dissolved remaining stake alongside Netflix
Seeking Alpha analysis: Berkshire sold UNH in Q1 2026 triggering analyst rating downgrade; stock slid on the 13F disclosure per Yahoo Finance report
